summary: Hezbollah launched multiple rocket attacks toward Metula and the broader confrontation line, while a Hezbollah drone struck the area where Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu was present, prompting his evacuation to a shelter. The IDF reported a mortar attack on a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon that killed a UN staff member and injured two others, and Israeli ground forces killed a Hezbollah gunman north of the Litani River, seizing weapons caches. Israeli airstrikes in Gaza eliminated several senior Hamas security officials, intensifying the conflict across multiple fronts.
key_points: Hezbollah fired two rockets toward Metula in northern Israel (Source: @pkpoi/142421).
A total of nine rockets were launched toward settlements along the confrontation line (Source: @pkpoi/142422).
A Hezbollah drone struck the area where Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was visiting the northern borders, forcing him to seek shelter (Source: @PressTV).
Hezbollah mortars hit a UNIFIL position in southern Lebanon, killing one UN staff member and wounding two others (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).

Israeli troops killed a Hezbollah operative armed with a Kalashnikov during ground operations north of the Litani River and seized dozens of weapons, including anti‑aircraft missiles (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
The Israeli Air Force struck two Hezbollah cells in Tyre and Shaqra, near Bint Jbeil (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Israeli airstrikes in the Gaza Strip killed senior Hamas security mechanism commanders Hassan Labad, Asem Shabir, Abdullah Abu Kaloub, and Mohammed Abu Marek (Source: @manniefabian/58409).

sections.executive_summary: Hezbollah launched multiple rockets toward Metula and the broader confrontation line, while a Hezbollah drone struck the area where Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu was present, prompting his evacuation to a shelter. The IDF reported a mortar attack on a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon that killed a UN staff member and injured two others, and Israeli ground forces killed a Hezbollah gunman north of the Litani River, seizing weapons caches. Israeli airstrikes in Gaza eliminated several senior Hamas security officials, intensifying the conflict across multiple fronts.

points: Israeli airstrikes in the Gaza Strip killed senior Hamas security mechanism officials Hassan Labad, Asem Shabir, Abdullah Abu Kaloub, and Mohammed Abu Marek, according to an IDF statement (Source: @manniefabian/58409).
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was forced to take shelter after a Hezbollah drone struck the area he was visiting in northern occupied Palestine (Source: @PressTV).

The IDF announced the appointment of Rear Adm. Tal Politis as the next defense attaché to the United States and the promotion of Brig. Gen. Barak Hiram to head the Operations Division (Source: @manniefabian/58412).

Israeli troops exchanged fire with a Hezbollah operative north of the Litani River, killing him and later raiding a weapons cache containing firearms and anti‑aircraft missiles (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Hezbollah launched two rockets toward Metula in northern Israel (Source: @pkpoi/142421).
A total of nine rockets were fired toward settlements along the confrontation line (Source: @pkpoi/142422).
Hezbollah mortars struck a UNIFIL post in southern Lebanon, killing one UN staff member and wounding two others (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).
Israeli forces killed a Hezbollah gunman armed with a Kalashnikov north of the Litani River and seized dozens of weapons, including anti‑aircraft missiles (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
The Israeli Air Force struck two Hezbollah cells in Tyre and Shaqra, near Bint Jbeil (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Kuwait reported that an Iranian drone attack on civilian facilities, including Kuwait International Airport, resulted in one death and 63 injuries (Source: @mamlekate/103613).

key_developments: Hezbollah fired two rockets toward Metula in northern Israel, prompting alerts (Source: @pkpoi/142421).
Nine rockets were launched toward settlements along the confrontation line, increasing northern Israel's threat level (Source: @pkpoi/142422).
A Hezbollah drone struck the area where Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was present during a northern border visit, forcing him to seek shelter (Source: @PressTV).
Hezbollah mortars hit a UNIFIL position in southern Lebanon, killing a UN staff member and injuring two others (Source: @idf_telegram/24134).
Israeli troops killed a Hezbollah operative north of the Litani River and seized dozens of weapons, including anti‑aircraft missiles (Source: @manniefabian/58404).
Israeli airstrikes in Gaza eliminated senior Hamas security mechanism commanders Hassan Labad, Asem Shabir, Abdullah Abu Kaloub, and Mohammed Abu Marek (Source: @manniefabian/58409).
Kuwait reported one civilian death and 63 injuries from an Iranian drone attack on civilian infrastructure, including the international airport (Source: @mamlekate/103613).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah's increased rocket and drone activity targeting northern Israel and UNIFIL positions indicates a heightened threat to Israeli civilian areas and international forces. The successful Israeli ground engagement and weapons seizure north of the Litani River demonstrate IDF capability to disrupt Hezbollah operations, yet the continued attacks suggest persistent escalation. Israeli airstrikes against senior Hamas figures in Gaza aim to degrade command structures but may provoke further retaliation across multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: Israel has responded with heightened alerts, increased air defense readiness, and targeted strikes against Hezbollah and Hamas assets, while also reshuffling senior military appointments to strengthen command. Lebanon's UNIFIL presence suffered casualties, potentially prompting diplomatic protests and calls for enhanced protection of peacekeepers. Kuwait's condemnation of the Iranian drone attack reflects broader Gulf concerns over Iranian proxy activities and may lead to coordinated security measures among Gulf states.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of rocket launch sites in southern Lebanon is critical, as recent launches indicate operational mobility for Hezbollah. The drone incident involving Prime Minister Netanyahu underscores the need for improved aerial surveillance and rapid response protocols in northern border zones. The capture of anti‑aircraft missiles in Hezbollah caches suggests a potential shift toward more advanced air defense capabilities by the group, warranting close observation of supply lines.
